Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack

Class MsgPackSerializer

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.OutputStreamSerializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack.MsgPackSerializer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Function<Object,byte[]>, ThrowingFunction<Object,byte[]>, MsgPackMetaProvider, ArrayRecordWritable, RecordWritable, TokenWritable
Direct Known Subclasses:
MsgPackSerializer.Base64, MsgPackSerializer.SpacedHex
public class MsgPackSerializer extends OutputStreamSerializer implements MsgPackMetaProvider, TokenWritable, ArrayRecordWritable
Serializes POJO models to MessagePack.
Media types:
Handles Accept types: application/msgpack, octal/msgpack

Produces Content-Type types: application/msgpack

The legacy media type octal/msgpack is retained in the Accept list as a backward-compatibility alias. The Content-Type emitted by the serializer is always the RFC-standard application/msgpack value.

Description

Java-to-MessagePack type mapping:

  • Maps and beans → MessagePack map type
  • Collections and arrays → MessagePack array type
  • String / Enum → MessagePack string type
  • Boolean → MessagePack boolean type
  • Number (int, long, float, double, etc.) → MessagePack number type (variable-length int or float)
  • Date / Calendar / Instant → ISO 8601 string
  • Duration → ISO 8601 duration string
  • byte[] → MessagePack binary type
  • null → MessagePack nil type
  • All other types → fallback to toString() as string
Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
See Also:
  • MessagePack Basics

    • createSession

      public MsgPackSerializerSession.Builder createSession()

      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class OutputStreamSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • getSession

      public MsgPackSerializerSession getSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Returns a session to use for this context.

      Note that subclasses may opt to return a reusable non-modifiable session.

      Overrides:
      getSession in class OutputStreamSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session object.

    • serializeTokens

      public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator that opens a MsgPackTokenWriter over the output using default session arguments (mirrors OutputStreamSerializer.serialize(Object)).

      The real implementation lives on MsgPackSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object). Callers that need request-derived configuration (locale, timezone, schema, swaps) should call createSession() and invoke MsgPackSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object) on the built session instead.

      Specified by:
      serializeTokens in interface TokenWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new MsgPackTokenWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • isAddBeanTypes

      protected final boolean isAddBeanTypes()
      Description copied from class: Serializer
      Add "_type" properties when needed.
      Overrides:
      isAddBeanTypes in class Serializer
      Returns:
      true if "_type" properties added to beans if their type cannot be inferred through reflection.
      See Also:
      • Serializer.Builder.addBeanTypes()

    • serializeArrayRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the buffered array-element RecordWriter (uses default session args; see serializeTokens(Object)). Real impl on MsgPackSerializerSession.serializeArrayRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      serializeArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A buffered RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.

    • isArrayRecordStreaming

      public boolean isArrayRecordStreaming()
      Description copied from interface: ArrayRecordWritable
      Streaming-quality signal queryable before opening a cursor.
      Specified by:
      isArrayRecordStreaming in interface ArrayRecordWritable
      Returns:
      true for true element streaming (json/cbor/msgpack); false for the buffered RecordAdapter.arrayWriter(org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSession, java.lang.Object)-backed formats (yaml, parquet, bson).

    • serializeArrayRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output, int expectedCount) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the streaming, count-prefixed array-element RecordWriter (uses default session args; see serializeTokens(Object)). Real impl on MsgPackSerializerSession.serializeArrayRecords(Object, int).
      Specified by:
      serializeArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output (must be an OutputStream).
      expectedCount - The number of elements that will be written.
      Returns:
      A streaming RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
